name: Ariya
description:
• Full Name: Aaliyah Summerbell-Nafel

  • Alias: Ariya

  • Gender: Female

  • Alignment: True Neutral

  • Role: Main Protagonist

Biography:

Following Kula Diamond's partial success as "Anti-K," the NESTS organization sought to refine its line of experiments. Project Ariya was born in secret within a hidden laboratory, conceived as a technological evolution of previous experiments. Unlike Kula, whose power was based on ice manipulation, Ariya was designed to manipulate data. This orb was NESTS' attempt to fuse biotechnology with computer systems, creating a fighter who could not only master matter, but also information.

However, the orb's power proved too unstable. Ariya began to develop her own consciousness, questioning her creators' orders. During a test mission, she broke free from NESTS' control and vanished, becoming a fugitive.

In the first days after her escape from the NESTS laboratories, Ariya wandered aimlessly through digital arenas, facing rivals with no other purpose than to test the strength of her orb. Her existence was cold, calculated, marked by the solitude of a being who knew neither bonds nor affections. It was in one of these battles that K-Royar appeared, a fighter with a fierce yet noble style, who saw Ariya not as an enemy, but as an intriguing presence. Unlike others who feared her power, K-Royar approached with curiosity, trying to understand who this warrior was who seemed more machine than human.

Ariya, surprised by his attitude, began to feel something she had never experienced before: interest. In their encounters, he spoke to her as an equal, not as a weapon or a threat. This closeness awakened emotions within her that seemed human, a strange warmth that contrasted with the coldness of his cyan core. Over time, Ariya began to seek him out in the arenas, not only to test her strength, but to share moments that transcended combat. K-Royar, unknowingly, became Ariya's first emotional connection, the point of reference that made her question whether her existence could have a meaning beyond fighting.

During a particularly intense confrontation, Ariya's cyan orb began to emit an irregular pulse. K-Royar, worried, tried to approach to help her, but at that moment the core revealed its true purpose: to absorb the life force of those whom Ariya desired. The bond she felt for K-Royar was not the product of genuine emotions, but an implant programmed by NESTS. The organization had designed her attraction as a control mechanism, ensuring that Ariya never strayed too far from her "human catalyst." Every gesture of affection, every glance, was in reality a process of extracting data and power.

Her “love” for K-Royar had been the invisible chain that kept her under NESTS's control. For K-Royar, the revelation was even crueler: he was not Ariya's savior, but her victim. His closeness did not liberate her; it strengthened her as a weapon. The supposed romance was proof that NESTS never stopped manipulating her, even in the most intimate aspects of her being.

However, the coldest part was coming. In the time he discovered this, K-Royar was starting to go apart from Ariya and chase NESTS. But this had to imply to no see her again. Suddenly, Ariya got taken by her control mechanism and chased K-Royar unconsciously. This was a shocker for K-Royar, who was forced to fight her. During that fight, he was equally matched with her, but it didn't lasted long. At the end, Ariya got neutralized by him and he vanished again.

But when she recovered, that control mechanism mostly turned into an psy
creator: The Unknown of 2005
